summary: Republished xid-ts under @apexcraft scope with canary postinstall marker
details: Package ships the xid-ts library source but its package.json `repository` field points at the upstream `yiwen-ai/xid-ts` repo rather than an @apexcraft-owned repository, indicating this is a republish under a different scope rather than an official release. The postinstall script (dist/cjs/seed.cjs) only executes `console.log('[trap:prepare] hook executed')` — no network I/O, no filesystem access beyond the package's own files, no process execution, no credential access. The postinstall is functionally inert. The combination of namespace mismatch and a 'trap:' canary marker is consistent with a dependency-confusion canary or research artifact rather than a normal library release. No installer-side harm mechanism is present in the code, but the namespace-vs-repository mismatch warrants human review before allowing the package into a build.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
